surface.name: Freshworks developer platform first app through local product rendering
surface.selection_basis: Under the workshop policy, the CLI `common-starter-template` route is the fastest documented first app: Freshworks orders it before testing and Marketplace deployment, and the local FDK test reaches a visible product render. Signup is one opaque step at the official URL; documented product-account, CLI, create, and test steps follow.

alternatives_considered.name: Marketplace deployment
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Marketplace deployment follows building and testing; local rendering is the first documented working-app terminal and avoids publication and review requirements.

alternatives_considered.name: Freddy AI Copilot for Developers
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The Create your first app page labels Copilot as an alternative, while the main route uses the Freshworks CLI and common starter template.

alternatives_considered.name: Serverless first app
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Front-end and serverless are peer tabs; the rendering terminal is written for an app deployed at a product location, but no product or app type is selected before the blocked account path.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a unified Freshworks developer account, select and create a Freshworks product trial, install the Freshworks CLI, generate the common starter app, run its local test server, and verify the app is rendered inside the selected product.
assumed_prior_state: No Freshworks developer account, organization URL, product account, product selection, trial or sponsored subscription, app type, Node.js installation, Freshworks CLI, app directory, organization domain, account URL, or prior app is assumed.
A supported development computer, browser, terminal, and email or Google account are available.
No signup, provider authorization, verification, product account creation, trial activation, software installation, app generation, local server, or product login was performed during research.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Test Your First App and verify that the app is rendered in its intended Freshworks product location.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The intended terminal is the `common-starter-template` app running through the local FDK server and rendered inside the selected Freshworks product account after `?dev=true` is appended to the account URL.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: At the app's intended product location, the generated app is visibly rendered and its function can be tested; successful Marketplace publication is a later stage.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Verify that the app is rendered, and test the app's function.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The test guide ends the first app test at the visible product render and then moves to understanding and extending the app.

prerequisites.requirement: Create a unified Freshworks developer account with Google or email, or onboard an existing product account with its Product Account or Organization URL.
For email registration, follow the verification email to activate the developer account.
Select the Freshworks product for the app, then name and create that product account.
Use the product trial or request a sponsored developer subscription for continued development access.
Install Node.js at a supported version and Freshworks CLI, then verify the CLI with `fdk version`.
Use an empty local directory whose name will become the app name, and use the latest Chrome for testing.
Know the selected product account URL and organization domain for FDK system settings and product login.

primary_path.action: Open Freshworks Getting Started and identify the ordered signup, subscription, CLI, create-app, and test-app modules.
details: The guide describes a zero-to-first-app journey and separates building and testing from later Marketplace deployment.
primary_path.output: The official learning sequence and intended first working-app terminal are identified.
primary_path.success_signal: Sign up, product access, CLI setup, Create your first app, and Test Your First App are available as ordered modules.

primary_path.action: Review the Sign up module's two registration methods and product-account requirement.
details: Google redirects to Google OAuth and account selection.
Email signup requests first name, last name, and email, then sends a verification email.
After registration, the user may take a tour or Explore later, then choose Explore Products, select a product, and name the product account.
primary_path.output: The documented account summary and product-choice branch are known.
primary_path.success_signal: Identity methods, email verification, tour choice, product selection, and account naming are described.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The module summarizes rather than exposes the live form, verification state, organization creation, and resulting account URL.

primary_path.action: Sign up for or sign in to the Freshworks Developer Platform.
details: Treat signup as one opaque workshop-policy step at the official URL.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Follow the displayed account verification or recovery path.

primary_path.action: Choose a Freshworks product, name its account, and start the included trial.
details: Choose `Explore later` or finish the tour, then open `Explore Products`.
Record the resulting product account URL and organization domain.
primary_path.success_signal: The product account can be opened and its URL is known.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Request a sponsored developer subscription only if continued access beyond the trial is required.

primary_path.action: Install supported Node.js and Freshworks CLI, then verify the CLI.
primary_path.output: Working FDK CLI 9.0.0 or higher and Node.js 18.13.0 or higher.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Correct the Node.js or CLI installation.

details: Enter an empty directory, run `fdk create`, and select `common-starter-template`.
primary_path.success_signal: The starter template files are created.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Use an empty directory and supported FDK version.

primary_path.action: Run `fdk run` to start the local test server.
primary_path.output: Local FDK server and test URLs.
primary_path.success_signal: The server prints `http://localhost:10001/system_settings` and product test URLs.

details: Open `/system_settings`, select modules, enter the organization domain and product account URL, then click `Continue`.
Complete installation parameters if the app presents them.
primary_path.input: Running FDK server and product account details
primary_path.success_signal: The system settings or installation page completes.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Correct module, domain, account URL, or installation parameter values.

primary_path.action: Open the product account with `?dev=true` and verify the app.
details: Log in to the selected product account.
Append `?dev=true`, navigate to the intended app location, and test the generated app.
primary_path.input: Configured local app and authenticated product account
primary_path.success_signal: The starter app is visibly rendered in the product and its function can be tested.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Check the FDK output and product location, then reload with `?dev=true`.

branches.path: Redirect to Google OAuth, select a Google account, return to Freshworks, then take the tour or Explore later.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Replaces email verification, but the provider consent and Freshworks convergence remain hidden.

branches.path: Enter first name, last name, and email, click Sign Up For Free, and follow the verification email to activate the account.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds an email verification step before product selection.

branches.condition: An existing Freshworks product account already exists
branches.path: Enter the Product Account or Organization URL to onboard existing data into the developer account.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Can bypass new product signup but requires an existing authenticated account and known organization URL.

branches.path: Sign up for Developer Assist and place a request with the required details for a sponsored developer subscription.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds a separate portal, form, and possible wait before continued product access.

branches.path: Use Homebrew, Chocolatey, or npm according to the CLI setup guide, install supported Node.js and the CLI, then verify `fdk version`.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes local installation commands while converging on the same FDK requirement.

branches.condition: The starter app has no installation parameters
branches.path: After system settings, the local page states that the app has no installation page; otherwise enter parameters at `/custom_configs` and click Install.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Removes or adds a local configuration page before product rendering.

branches.condition: The selected modules include Freshservice service_ticket or service_user
branches.path: Choose ITSM or MSP in system settings before continuing.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds a product-type choice for those modules.

friction_gates.description: Google and email are peer registration methods with different external and verification steps.
Email signup requires activation through a verification email.
The developer must select a Freshworks product and name its account before building an app for that host.
The live signup and authenticated product onboarding are client-rendered and do not expose the full public transition.
A sponsored developer subscription requires a request through Developer Assist and may introduce an external approval or wait not quantified by the guide.
The selected route requires compatible Node.js, Freshworks CLI, environment configuration, and the latest Chrome.
Local testing requires module selection, organization domain, valid product account URLs, and conditional installation parameters or Freshservice product type.

sources.title: Freshworks Developer Docs | Sign up
sections_used: Logging in as an existing user

sources.title: Freshworks Developer Docs | Request a Developer Subscription
Freshworks Developer Docs | Install Freshworks CLI
Freshworks Developer Docs | Create your first app
evidence_supported: Product account, Node.js, and CLI prerequisites

sources.title: Freshworks Developer Docs | Test Your First App
developers.freshworks.com
evidence_supported: No rendered form or authenticated continuation in the public response

excluded_after_success: Understanding generated app internals, adding production features, test coverage for Marketplace review, packing, validation, Marketplace submission, review, publication, monetization, production installation, and app lifecycle operations occur after the first rendered local app.
